steps.name: Reconcile Philosophy record, implement validate_philosophy_json, embed seeds
steps.description: Reconcile the stale lexicon::philosophy::Philosophy record to the ADR-059 schema, implement the per-field-gated pure validator mirroring validate_claim_json, add the pure object_id/normalize functions, and embed >=10 well-known seed records via include_str! with a public accessor plus a seed-validity/no-collision unit test. This is the pure core: no signer, no store, no network.
steps.implementation_notes: Reconcile Philosophy from {id, label, description} to {name: String, description: String, aliases: Vec<String> (serde default), see_also: Vec<String> (serde rename "seeAlso", default)} — drop id/label. Implement validate_philosophy_json as a per-field-gated pure fn mirroring crates/lexicon/src/claim.rs::validate_claim_json: required name/description missing -> LexiconError::MissingField{field}; aliases/seeAlso not array-of-string -> LexiconError::InvalidType; serde catch-all -> LexiconError::SchemaMismatch. REUSE the existing LexiconError enum — do NOT create a parallel error type. object_id(name) = "org.openlore.philosophy." + normalize(name); normalize: lowercase -> trim -> collapse whitespace/underscore runs to '-' -> keep [a-z0-9-] only. Object id is DERIVED, never stored on the record. Ship >=10 seeds via include_str!("seeds.json") (mirror PHILOSOPHY_LEXICON_JSON pattern) as unsigned constants: the six hard-pinned names (memory-safety, type-safety, test-driven, documentation-first, dependency-pinning, semantic-versioning) plus >=4 more (e.g. reproducible-builds, local-first, backward-compatibility, minimalism, immutability, federation-first), each with a real one-paragraph description and optional aliases. Expose a public seeds() accessor. Add a unit test that validates every seed through validate_philosophy_json AND asserts no two seed names collide under normalize. LX-reject asserts LexiconError::MissingField{field:"description"}. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step's pure seams (validate_philosophy_json, normalize, object_id) MUST be property-based (proptest) with example-based as documented FALLBACK. Acceptance scenarios are the pre-authored subprocess tests (example-based, real-io) — do NOT rewrite them.

steps.name: philosophy list verb — human text discovery surface (offline)
steps.description: Add Command::Philosophy(PhilosophyCommand::List{json}) to the clap enum, create verbs/philosophy_list.rs (registered in verbs/mod.rs), and wire dispatch. The text default view reads the embedded seeds and prints, per seed, the derived object id + name + one-line description. Offline by construction: no store, no network handle. This is the walking skeleton plus the reinforcing count/backward-compat/default/offline scenarios, all satisfied by the single text-output implementation.
criteria: AC-001.1 / AC-001.2 / AC-001.4 / I-9 / KPI-PV-1 / ADR-059 D1 (dotted-lowercase NSID backward-compat)
steps.implementation_notes: Add Command::Philosophy(PhilosophyCommand::List{json: bool}) to the clap enum in crates/cli/src/lib.rs. Create verbs/philosophy_list.rs mirroring the existing verb structure — return (exit_code: i32, stdout: String); do NOT write stdout directly, the dispatcher prints. Register the verb in verbs/mod.rs and wire it into crates/cli/src/lib.rs::dispatch. The text default view iterates lexicon::philosophy::seeds(), and for each prints the DERIVED object_id(name) + name + one-line description. PV-3 requires the derived object id to match the slice-1 claim object bytes exactly (dotted lowercase NSID, ADR-059 D1). PV-2/KPI-PV-1 require >=10 distinct seeds visible in output. PV-6/I-9 require success with the network disabled — offline by construction, no store or network dependency. Keep xtask check-arch at 20 workspace members (+xtask = 21): NO new crate. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step's pure seams (validate_philosophy_json, normalize, object_id) MUST be property-based (proptest) with example-based as documented FALLBACK. Acceptance scenarios are the pre-authored subprocess tests (example-based, real-io) — do NOT rewrite them.

steps.name: philosophy list --json opt-in array emission
steps.description: Add the --json opt-in branch to the philosophy list verb: when json is true, emit a JSON array of the full records (each with name and description). Text remains the default; --json is strictly opt-in.
steps.implementation_notes: Extend verbs/philosophy_list.rs so that when the --json flag is set, the verb serializes the seed records as a JSON array — each element carrying name and description (and serde-shaped aliases/seeAlso where present). Text output from 02-01 stays the DEFAULT path; --json only changes the emission, not the data source. Still offline by construction. PV-4 asserts each record appears in the JSON array with name and description. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step's pure seams (validate_philosophy_json, normalize, object_id) MUST be property-based (proptest) with example-based as documented FALLBACK. Acceptance scenarios are the pre-authored subprocess tests (example-based, real-io) — do NOT rewrite them.

validation.review_verdict: APPROVED (nw-software-crafter-reviewer, 0 blockers / 0 should-fix / 0 nits) + automated quality gate PASS (decomposition ratio 0.5 steps/production-files, no orphans, all 8 RED tests owned once, valid linear DAG 01-01 -> 02-01 -> 02-02, step IDs valid, no private-method AC coupling).
